source: Amnesty International Security Lab — Forensic Methodology Report: How to catch NSO Group's Pegasus (2021)
note: Starter subset only. For a real investigation load Amnesty's full pegasus.stix2 (~700+ domains, more processes/emails) via --iocs or ISCOUT_STIX2/MVT_STIX2. Pegasus rotates process names, so absence of these does NOT rule out infection.

indicators.description: First-stage 'BridgeHead' payload observed in DataUsage after visits to Pegasus install domains.
Pegasus process name (spoofs a system daemon).

Pegasus process name from the Amnesty appendix. Scored medium (WARNING) because it collides with the legitimate iOS daemon com.apple.protectedcloudstorage.pcsd — treat as a lead needing corroboration (orphaned usage / install-domain visit), not proof.
Pegasus process name from the Amnesty appendix. Scored medium (WARNING) — name may collide with a legitimate Find-My-style daemon; corroborate before treating as compromise.
Pegasus process name (spoofs iOS keyroll daemon naming). Scored medium (WARNING) — may collide with a legitimate CloudKit daemon; corroborate before treating as compromise.
Pegasus process name masquerading as a system service. Scored medium (WARNING) because 'gatekeeperd' is a real Apple daemon name; corroborate before treating as compromise.
Pegasus process name listed in the Amnesty report appendix.
Pegasus process name listed in the Amnesty report appendix.
Pegasus V4 exploitation/installation domain published by Amnesty.
Pegasus infrastructure domain published by Amnesty.
Pegasus infrastructure domain published by Amnesty.
Pegasus staging directory holding malicious binaries (not present on clean devices).
